scope_advisory_reference: automation_bus/latest_pipeline_advisory.md — Post-P2-1 Pipeline Throughput Review (2026-06-27). Advisory recommends P2-2+P2-3 combined STANDARD/CONTENT sprint as next sprint. Advisory validity confirmed: git log --since=2026-06-27 returns no commits to advisory-cited files.

changes.evidence: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:2-6 — work_id:P2-2+P2-3, branch:feature/p2-2-p2-3-retail-pathway-explainer-expansion, risk_level:STANDARD, execution_model:SINGLE_PHASE, change_type:CONTENT. All five mandatory fields present.

All files in scope are Knowledge Bus prose assets (YAML content files) and tests. No Intelligence Core files (narrative compilers, signal evaluator, domain assembler, pipeline logic, payload/report schemas) appear in scope. Explicit files-out-of-scope list at automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:94-117 names every IC module by path. STANDARD confirmed under SOP §3/§10: no IC, no ssot/, no pipeline/, no scoring policy files touched.

Sprint adds/extends YAML content in retail explainer registry (backend/ssot/retail_explainer_v1/registry.yaml) and pathway explainer pack (knowledge_bus/pathway_explainers_v1/pathway_explainers_v1.yaml). No Python runtime behaviour changes. Conditional P1-26 M1 correction is header metadata naming only — package_id field value update in YAML headers, not signal logic. CONTENT classification holds.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:1-401 — seven sequential phases, no gated activation switch, no allowlist toggle separating content authoring from content activation. Content YAML files are loaded directly at runtime without a feature flag gate requiring a deferred second phase. SINGLE_PHASE correct.

Both P2-2 (retail biomarker explainers in backend/ssot/retail_explainer_v1/registry.yaml) and P2-3 (pathway/missing-marker explainers in knowledge_bus/pathway_explainers_v1/) target non-overlapping YAML files within the same Knowledge Bus prose surface. Same risk class, same change_type, same execution agent, no cross-file dependency that would require phased separation. Combination is structurally sound.

backend/core/ssot/retail_explainer_registry_v1.py:21 — _registry_path() resolves to 'backend/ssot/retail_explainer_v1/registry.yaml'. File confirmed present with 17 biomarker entries (glucose, hba1c, insulin, total_cholesterol, ldl_cholesterol, hdl_cholesterol, triglycerides, non_hdl_cholesterol, apob, creatinine, egfr, alt, ast, hemoglobin, ferritin, vitamin_d, tsh) and 10 system entries. Coverage target of ≥40 biomarker entries requires authoring at least 23 new entries.

backend/tests/enforcement/test_retail_explainer_registry_b1b.py:51-59 — test_retail_explainer_registry_b1b_keys_match_ssot() loads backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml and asserts every key in registry.yaml biomarkers section exists in biomarkers.yaml. Any key added to registry.yaml that is absent from biomarkers.yaml will cause an enforcement test failure and block the finish gate. Hardening addition: Phase 1 must explicitly read backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml to extract all canonical biomarker_ids before Phase 2 authoring begins. Cursor must cross-check every planned new key against this list. Record confirmed keys and any gaps (e.g. a desired marker absent from biomarkers.yaml cannot be added to the registry). Do not add registry entries for non-canonical ids.

backend/tests/enforcement/test_retail_explainer_registry_b1b.py:69-70 — test_retail_explainer_registry_b1b_nonempty_educational_text() checks body.lower() contains 'general education' or 'educational'. Every new biomarker entry body must include one of these phrases. Existing entries confirm the closing sentence pattern: 'This is general education only. It is not a diagnosis and does not replace advice from a qualified clinician about your own results.' Hardening addition: all 23+ new biomarker entries must carry this closing sentence verbatim or equivalent. This is not optional — it is an enforcement gate check.

backend/tests/unit/test_pathway_explainers_v1.py:32 — test_four_benchmark_pathways_present() asserts len(pathways) == 4 exactly. Current four IDs: one_carbon_methylation_homocysteine_v1, lipid_transport_cholesterol_handling_v1, blood_iron_oxygen_handling_v1, thyroid_hormone_antibody_context_v1. Adding any new pathway entry to pathway_explainers_v1.yaml will break this assertion and fail Phase 5 validation. Hardening addition: Phase 5 must update test_four_benchmark_pathways_present to assert the new pathway count and updated ID set. Do not weaken the test — replace the exact-count and exact-ID assertions with updated values. This is a mandatory test update, not a test weakening.

knowledge_bus/pathway_explainers_v1/pathway_explainers_v1.yaml confirmed to contain blood_iron_oxygen_handling_v1 and thyroid_hormone_antibody_context_v1 as existing entries. Phase 3 additions for blood/iron/oxygen and thyroid domains must UPDATE these existing entries (extend or enrich field content) rather than add duplicate pathway_ids. New entries may be created only for kidney and homocysteine/cardiovascular context (if governed) where no existing pathway_id exists. Cursor must check existing pathway_ids before authoring to prevent duplication.

backend/tests/unit/test_pathway_explainers_v1.py:77-81 — test_no_giant_unstructured_only_blob() checks len(text_fields) >= 8 per pathway (text fields excluding display_title). Existing entries confirm each pathway uses named fields: pathway_role, system_in_action, markers_belong_together, why_matters_beyond_a_single_marker_reading, interpretive_caution, and domain-specific fields (e.g. homocysteine_in_this_pathway). Any new or updated pathway entry must follow this multi-field schema with at least 8 named text fields — not a single body key.

Glob search for knowledge_bus/**/*missing* and backend/**/*missing_marker* returns no files. No missing_marker_explainer YAML file or directory exists anywhere in the repository. Phase 3 references 'N-7 missing-marker entries' as if an existing registry is being extended, but this is new infrastructure. Hardening addition: Phase 3 missing-marker work must bootstrap a new governed file. Before creating this file, Cursor must: (1) determine the appropriate location and schema consistent with the pathway_explainers_v1 pattern; (2) create a companion test file for the new structure; (3) apply the same STOP gate — if content boundaries cannot safely govern the proposed missing-marker entry, defer. If missing-marker bootstrap is blocked, record in carry-forward and continue with retail and pathway work. Do not invent a schema without a governed source anchor.

Grep for KBP-473 across knowledge_bus/ returns 13 files, not 3. All 13 are pkg_kb52c_* signal_library.yaml files with package_id: KBP-473x in the library header. The three iron files are: pkg_kb52c_iron_low_absolute_iron_deficiency/signal_library.yaml (KBP-4731), pkg_kb52c_iron_low_functional_iron_restriction_inflammation/signal_library.yaml, pkg_kb52c_iron_high_iron_overload_context/signal_library.yaml. The remaining 10 files contain KBP-473x naming across basophil, apob, and bilirubin packages. Hardening addition: this sprint may correct ONLY the three iron files if the sprint touches the iron explainer source area. Do not correct the remaining 10 basophil/apob/bilirubin files — this would broaden scope beyond the conditional gate. Phase 6 carry-forward must explicitly record 'M1-adjacent naming defect: KBP-473x persists in 10 non-iron pkg_kb52c_* signal_library.yaml files — requires a separate housekeeping sprint.' The SOP prompt's phrase 'three iron signal_library.yaml headers' is architecturally correct as a scope limit, not an error.

docs/architecture/RETAIL_EXPLAINER_CONTENT_BOUNDARIES_v1.md confirmed present. It defines: BiomarkerEducationalExplainerV1 must be reusable, non-personalised, must not state patient-specific conclusions. SystemEducationalExplainerV1 must read as general education, not proof of individual illness. Symptom relevance is deferred and must not appear. Contribution context is cluster membership only — no mechanism/causality/symptom inference. Source SSOT file is backend/ssot/retail_explainer_v1/registry.yaml. These boundaries are fully reflected in acceptance criteria at automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:350-355. No reference ranges, no diagnosis, no treatment advice in retail entries — confirmed and covered.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:94-117 — files-out-of-scope explicitly names all IC modules: narrative_report_compiler_v1.py, narrative_compiler_lc_s3_assembly_v1.py, narrative_payload_builder_v1.py, narrative_brief_enforcement_v1.py, narrative_payload_v1.py, narrative_report_v1.py, domain_score_assembler.py, signal_evaluator.py, scoring policy files, signal activation files, parser files, questionnaire files, frontend files, Gemini files, payload/report schema files, compiled card routing files (except read-only reference), production PSI package files (except read-only reference), calculated TSAT files, WBC/lymphocyte/neutrophil files, TPOAb euthyroid or TgAb deferred files. Scope boundary correctly drawn.

git log --since=2026-06-27 -- automation_bus/latest_pipeline_advisory.md knowledge_bus/pathway_explainers_v1/pathway_explainers_v1.yaml backend/ssot/retail_explainer_v1/registry.yaml returns no output (no commits since advisory date). Advisory claims for current build position, coverage count (~17/79 biomarkers), and candidate sprint sequencing are valid and may be inherited per CLAUDE.md §14.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:44,112-117 — P2-FRAME-ROUTING-ARCHITECTURE-1 (HIGH/BEHAVIOUR, frame-level lead entity discrimination), P2-4 NarrativePayload hardening (HIGH/BEHAVIOUR), Gemini activation (P4-1/P4-2, requires P2-4 and CEO approval), calculated TSAT (Core Engine derived-metric governance), WBC/lymphocyte/neutrophil (Core Engine SSOT canonical identity) are all explicitly out of scope. Advisory latest_pipeline_advisory.md:113-119 confirms blocked status for each. No sprint action required.

advisory_inheritance_status.escalation_rationale: No escalation required. Stage D verification confirms no Intelligence Core files in scope. Advisory risk classification is correct and unchanged.
advisory_inheritance_status.material_departure_from_advisory: Four hardening additions beyond SOP draft scope: (1) Biomarker key SSOT gate — every new registry key must be verified against backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml before authoring; enforcement test at test_retail_explainer_registry_b1b.py:51 is a live gate; (2) Pathway test count update — test_pathway_explainers_v1.py:32 asserts len==4 and must be updated to reflect new pathway count after Phase 3 additions; (3) Missing-marker infrastructure does not exist — Phase 3 must bootstrap new governed file from scratch, not extend an existing registry; (4) M1 scope clarification — KBP-473x naming defect affects 13 files, not 3; sprint corrects only the 3 iron files; 10 others must be recorded in carry-forward.

overall_verdict: HARDENED. All five front-matter fields confirmed valid. STANDARD/CONTENT/SINGLE_PHASE classification confirmed — no Intelligence Core files in scope, no runtime behaviour changes, no allowlist gate requiring two-phase separation. P2-2+P2-3 combination is structurally sound. Advisory validity confirmed (no commits since 2026-06-27). Retail explainer registry confirmed at 17 biomarker entries; ≥40 target requires minimum 23 new entries. Four hardening additions: (1) all new biomarker keys must be pre-verified against backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml to pass enforcement gate at test_retail_explainer_registry_b1b.py:51; (2) all new entry bodies must include 'general education' or 'educational' phrase to pass enforcement gate at test_retail_explainer_registry_b1b.py:69; (3) test_pathway_explainers_v1.py:32 asserts len==4 and must be updated in Phase 5 after new pathway entries are added; (4) missing-marker explainer infrastructure does not exist and must be bootstrapped as a new governed file with companion tests. M1 scope confirmed: 3 iron files only; 10 other KBP-473x files must be deferred to carry-forward. Sprint cleared for kernel start.
